data.title: Citi holds firm on S&P 500 target despite Iran tensions and the current market pullback
data.description:
Citigroup is standing by its year-end outlook for the S&P 500 (SP500), even as mounting geopolitical tensions and broader macroeconomic risks weigh on investor sentiment and push equities lower in 2026.

In a recent note to clients, the bank reiterated its base-case target of 7,700 for the benchmark index, a forecast underpinned by projected earnings of roughly $320 per share—an estimate it now views as potentially conservative. Citi also outlined a more optimistic scenario of 8,300, driven by stronger-than-expected profit growth and modest valuation expansion. On the downside, a bearish case of 5,700 reflects the possibility of weaker fundamentals and declining multiples. 

The firm acknowledged that the ongoing Iran-related conflict presents a fresh challenge to its outlook, adding to an already complex landscape shaped by artificial intelligence disruption risks, concerns within private credit markets, and lingering uncertainty around global trade policy. 

Despite these headwinds, Citi has not yet adjusted its projections, stating: “We maintain our full year targets for now.”

The S&P 500 is currently trading near 6,417, down approximately 6.7% year to date. Achieving the bank’s base-case target would require a gain of about 20% from current levels, underscoring the extent of the potential rebound implied in its forecast. 

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: Crypto stocks, U, VERI, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Friday as President Donald Trump’s decision to extend a critical deadline for strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure failed to assuage investor anxiety. The reprieve was seen by many as a temporary stay rather than a permanent resolution, leaving markets vulnerable to continued volatility and a high geopolitical risk premium.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Argan (AGX) +33% – Shares advanced after the company reported strong Q4 results, with revenue rising 13% Y/Y and both gross and EBITDA margins expanding by 450 bps. The company also highlighted robust demand, adding $2.5B in new contracts during the year and ending with a project backlog exceeding $2.9B, supporting continued growth and profitability momentum.
  • ADMA Biologics (ADMA) +14% – Shares rebounded after the company pushed back against allegations in a Culper Research short report, stating that additional data on its immune globulin portfolio, including inventory levels and demand trends, refutes the claims. The company said accusations of channel stuffing reflect a misunderstanding of IVIG market dynamics. The response follows a ~27% drop in the stock since the short report was published earlier in the week.
  • Unity Software (U) +8% – Shares surged after the company raised its FQ1 outlook, projecting revenue of $505M–$508M, above prior guidance of $480M–$490M and ahead of expectations. Growth is being driven by strength in its Unity Vector offering, with Create revenue seen at ~$115M and Grow at ~$353M. Adjusted EBITDA is also expected to come in stronger at $130M–$135M, topping earlier forecasts.

Biggest stock losers

  • Veritone (VERI) -23% – Shares declined after the company reported weak preliminary Q4 results, with revenue expected at $18.1M–$30.0M, well below the $34.3M consensus. Preliminary GAAP net loss is seen at $25.1M–$37.0M, while non-GAAP net loss is estimated at $2.0M–$13.9M. For FY2026, Veritone guided revenue to $130M–$145M (vs. $129.3M consensus) and expects a narrower non-GAAP net loss of $13.5M–$22.5M.
  • Zenas BioPharma (ZBIO) -19% – Shares declined after the company announced a dual capital raise, including $200M in 2.50% convertible senior notes due 2032 and a 5M-share equity offering priced at $20 per share. Net proceeds are expected to total ~$287.5M, with additional greenshoe options for both offerings. The notes will be unsecured, carry a 2.50% coupon, and mature in April 2032. Both offerings are slated to close on March 31, 2026.
  • MARA Holdings (MARA) -8% - Crypto-linked equities retreated sharply Friday as Bitcoin slid 5% to $65.6K, triggering a broad selloff across the digital asset ecosystem. The downward momentum weighed heavily on the sector, dragging MicroStrategy (MSTR) down 6% and Coinbase (COIN) more than 7%, while mining peer Bitfarms (BITF) fell 5%. The selling pressure intensified across the broader infrastructure space, with CleanSpark (CLSK), Riot Platforms (RIOT), Iren (IREN), and TeraWulf (WULF) all surrendering over 8%. This retreat comes as a combination of wider market weakness and persistent geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continues to dampen risk appetite for high-beta assets.

data.title: Cruise stocks pressured on Carnival's warning higher fuel costs will weigh on FY26 results
data.description:
Shares of Carnival Corp. (CCL) are underwater at Friday’s open as record fiscal first-quarter results that included record bookings for the quarter and a long-term goal of 50% EPS growth were overshadowed by the company’s expectation that higher fuel prices will weigh on its full-year results.

“Given the recent spike and volatility in fuel prices, we believe it is reasonable to forecast fuel based on the purchased price for the month of March and early April,” the company said.  

The company now expects to earn an adjusted profit of $2.21 per share, down from its earlier guidance of $2.48 per share and less than $2.36 estimates.

Adjusted EBITDA is seen at $7.2B versus $7.6B previously.

Excluding fuel, overall costs are seen lower this year as the company guided adjusted cruise costs per available lower berth days ((ALBD)) up 3.1% year-over-year (constant currency) versus a prior outlook of up 3.25%.

While the company will consume the same amount of fuel this year, the cost swelled to $718 per metric ton compared to an earlier estimate of $524, raising its fuel expense to $2.15B in FY26 from an earlier estimate of $1.63B.

For the current fiscal quarter, Carnival (CCL) expects an adjusted profit of $0.34 per share, less than $0.39 per share estimates.

For the reported quarter, however, Carnival beat both profit and sales expectations with increases of 54% and 6.2%, respectively, year-over-year. Bookings for 2026 are already surpassing prior records, up by double digits “strengthening the company’s record booked position at historically high prices.”

Carnival (CCL) also announced PROPEL, a set of long-term targets designed to reflect continued earnings growth momentum, outsized shareholder distribution, and higher returns to be achieved by 2029. PROPEL goals include a 16%+ return on invested capital, 50% adjusted EPS growth from 2025, and 40%+ distributions to shareholders.

Finally, the board approved an additional $2.5B share buyback program.

Pressure on Carnival (CCL) shares is reverberating through the sector, with Royal Caribbean (RCL) and Viking Holdings (VIK) both down 2.7% and Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) lower by 4.6%.

data.title: Netflix price hike to drive revenue upside, boost forecast - analysts
data.description:
Oppenheimer remains bullish on Netflix (NFLX) after the streaming pioneer announced price hikes for its subscription plans to contribute the additional revenue towards its ambitious $20B content spending budget.

The equity research firm believes Netflix continues to dominate the streaming market due to its upper hand in producing high-engagement content and its effective monetization strategy when compared to peers.

The price hikes in the U.S. and Canada alone will be more than 3% incremental to the company's 2026 revenue, the research firm said.

"NFLX's ability to retain consumers drives its industry-low churn and builds its competitive content moat," Oppenheimer said in its note dated March 26. "We only see more opportunity for NFLX to lean into content post-WBD/PSKY merger."

The research firm noted that engagement trends from Q3 and Q4 gave the company confidence to raise its subscription plan prices, and the upside from hikes in Portugal and Argentina will be minor. They continue to maintain “outperform” but raised the price target by $10 to $135, implying a 44.7% growth potential.

Citi, on the other hand, also maintained its rating at “buy” and expects price hikes to result in a ~2.5% tailwind relative to FY26 guidance, with scope for the company to raise prices in Europe in the coming months.

Additionally, they expect a beat-and-raise Q1 quarterly report for the company. PT was unchanged at $115, implying a 23.2% upside.

NFLX is down 0.75% so far this year and fell 4.1% in the past 12 months.

data.title: DraftKings is the worst performing large-cap consumer discretionary stock YTD
data.description:
U.S. large-cap consumer discretionary stocks have struggled year-to-date in 2026, with the sector underperforming the broader market amid weakening consumer spending and tariff concerns.

Below is a list of the 10 worst-performing large-cap consumer discretionary stocks with market capitalizations over $10B, ranked by their year-to-date performance.

The list is topped by DraftKings Inc. (DKNG), with a YTD performance down 38.28%. DoorDash, Inc. (DASH) and Carvana Co. (CVNA) are next, with declines of 32.90% and 28.59% respectively.

Rivian Automotive, Inc. (RIVN) and Coupang, Inc. (CPNG) round out the bottom five, with YTD declines of 22.83% and 22.13%. The list represents a variety of industries, including Casinos and Gaming, Restaurants, Automotive Retail, and Automobile Manufacturers.

Here is the list:

  1. DraftKings Inc. (DKNG), YTD perf: -38.28%, Quant Rating: 2.61

  2. DoorDash, Inc. (DASH), YTD perf: -32.90%, Quant Rating: 2.62

  3. Carvana Co. (CVNA), YTD perf: -28.59%, Quant Rating: 2.87

  4. Rivian Automotive, Inc. (RIVN), YTD perf: -22.83%, Quant Rating: 2.88

  5. Coupang, Inc. (CPNG), YTD perf: -22.13%, Quant Rating: 2.74

  6. Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG), YTD perf: -21.32%, Quant Rating: 2.99

  7. Las Vegas Sands Corp. (LVS), YTD perf: -19.50%, Quant Rating: 3.50

  8. Chewy, Inc. (CHWY), YTD perf: -18.28%, Quant Rating: 2.99

  9. NIKE, Inc. (NKE), YTD perf: -18.27%, Quant Rating: 3.61

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI)

data.title: Carnival in charts: Passengers carried reaches 3.10M in FQ1; occupancy at 103%
Entergy surges after Meta signs deal to fund new gas plants for Louisiana AI data center
data.description:
Entergy (ETR) up 6.2% in early trading Friday to lead the S&P 500 leaderboard after saying Meta Platforms (META) agreed to pay for the construction of seven new natural gas-fired plants to provide a total of 5.2 GW of electricity for the massive data center it is developing in rural Louisiana.

To support the expansion of the Hyperion data center in Richland Parish, Entergy (ETR) said Meta (META) will pay for a comprehensive buildout of seven gas plants, three of which have already been approved by state regulators, and 240 miles of transmission lines connecting south Louisiana to north Louisiana and Arkansas, battery energy storage, and nuclear power uprates.

Entergy (ETR) said the deal was "structured to ensure Meta pays its full cost of service" and that the agreement would deliver more than $2B in customer savings over 20 years; Meta did not comment on how much it would spend on the new gas plants and other infrastructure related to the project.

Entergy (ETR) received approval last year to build three natural gas plants that will generate 2.3 GW of electricity for Meta's data center.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: DIS, SNDK, SJM, OWL
data.description:
Disney (DIS) and J. M. Smucker (SJM) have both received upgrades from Seeking Alpha analysts, with Paul Franke highlighting long-term value in Disney during economic shifts and oil price spikes. Vladimir Dimitrov, CFA, suggests that Smucker’s record-low valuation offers an opportunity despite a history of capital allocation missteps and brand underperformance. On the downgrade side, Blue Owl Capital (OWL) and Sandisk Corporation (SNDK) have seen their ratings lowered due to shifting industry sentiment and technical pressures. 

Upgrades

  • The J. M. Smucker Company (SJM): Upgrade to Cautious Rating by Vladimir Dimitrov, CFA. The analyst notes that while the company faces ongoing asset impairments and brand challenges, the current record-low valuation presents a potential turning point.

    “The stock is priced at record low levels, while we are already seeing some early signs of a recovery. It is still quite early to assess whether or not these trends will continue, but given SJM's conservative valuation, I see a reason to become cautiously optimistic about the stock and upgrade it to a Buy.”

  • The Walt Disney Company (DIS): Upgrade to Buy by Paul Franke. The analyst points to historical data suggesting that Disney offers compelling value when purchased during oil price peaks and recessionary selloffs, projecting significant upside through 2028.

    “To me, the developing economic and stock valuation situation reminds me of the 1990 Persian Gulf War recession plus the late 2008 peak in crude oil and Great Recession span. If this reflection proves correct, acquiring DIS shares under $95, possibly falling back to $80 or lower in the months ahead, may be the most productive course of action for those with a holding period target of 3-5 years or longer.”

Downgrades

  • Blue Owl Capital (OWL): Downgrade Strong Buy to Hold by Deep Value Investing. Although fundamental damage is not yet visible, the analyst is stepping back due to negative sentiment and redemption pressures currently facing the broader private credit industry.

    “I am no longer confident in a turnaround this year, and I downgrade to a Hold. I am not downgrading to a Sell, as I haven't seen any clear signs to suggest a material deterioration in the company's fundamentals. Therefore, I wouldn't short OWL stock at this price level.”

  • Sandisk Corporation (SNDK): Downgrade to Sell by The Techie. After a massive 1,100% rally, the stock is facing technical weakness and potential disruption to its AI-driven growth narrative from Google’s TurboQuant algorithm.

    “I think the stock giving out steam at current levels is precisely what I expected at some point. After a 1,100% run over the past year and a fresh all-time high just days ago, the stock was priced for perfection, and perfection is a fragile thing to hold."

data.title: The bar for interest rate hikes is quite high as growth slows – JPM’s Oksana Aronov
data.description:
The Federal Reserve faces a high bar to raise interest rates despite market speculation pricing in significant odds of a January hike, according to Oksana Aronov, head of market strategy for alternative fixed income at JPMorgan Asset Management.

In an interview with CNBC, Aronov acknowledged that while the economy is showing signs of slowing growth, the case for aggressive policy tightening remains difficult to make.

“The bar for hikes is quite high, especially now that we’re seeing some slowing of growth,” she said.

Aronov was critical of the Fed’s previous monetary policy decisions, arguing that interest rate cuts were “premature” and that the central bank should have stayed put.

She expressed concern that loose monetary policy “fueled more issues in the private credit market” by making financing too accessible. The vulnerabilities in private credit, she noted, are “slowly revealing” themselves rather than manifesting as an immediate crash.

When it comes to navigating the yield curve, Aronov favors the front end for picking up yield while avoiding longer-term interest rate risk. She explained that the long end of the curve responds primarily to three factors: inflation, the fiscal backdrop, and treasury supply needed to address the deficit.

“More Treasury issuance as a result of that is going to put a cap on any rally there in the absence of a really meaningful, significant recession,” she said.

The strategist warned that the full impact of geopolitical risks and the oil shock has yet to be felt across the broader economy. She pointed to industries like petrochemicals and fertilizers that will face significant pressure from elevated oil prices.

“Spot oil prices differ around the world and, by some estimates, are as high as $160 in Asia,” Aronov noted, highlighting the gap between futures markets and reality on the ground.

In this environment, Aronov emphasized the importance of diversification, noting that investors “are learning that lesson the hard way right now as both stocks and bonds are falling” in a “highly correlated way.”

Her team has maintained a significant liquidity buffer and recommends credit protection, which she described as “extremely inexpensive” given that “we’re in the last throes of a credit cycle here.”

Despite the challenging macro backdrop, Aronov sees the U.S. as better positioned than its global peers.

She warned that Europe faces a more difficult situation, with “possible rationings of fuel” if current geopolitical conflicts persist.

“The U.S. is still better positioned to weather this than Europe for sure,” she concluded.

Treasury ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), and (BIL).

Bond ETFs: (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), and (HYG).

Inflation Protection ETFs: (VTIP), (TIP), (SCHP), (STIP), (TIPX), (SPIP), (WIP), (GTIP), (LQDI), and (RINF).

Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL).

data.title: Despite recent drawdowns, BTC remains a resilient asset rather than a pure hedge – analyst
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has emerged as a surprisingly resilient asset during the first month of the war in the Middle East, outperforming both gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and technology stocks (VGT), (XLK), (IYW).

The cryptocurrency was up approximately 5% over the past month at the start of Friday—although it has declined 3.8% to $66,173—while gold (XAUUSD:CUR) has fallen around 14.4% and the Nasdaq has dropped roughly 6.5%.

In an interview with CNBC, Gazmararian noted that Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) digital nature gives it unique advantages during periods of geopolitical turmoil.

“A natively digital asset holds a lot of appeal because it can move across borders,” she explained, adding that investors can maintain self-custody of Bitcoin (BTC-USD) rather than relying on the global financial system amid widespread sanctions or gold reserves held in potentially inaccessible foreign vaults.

The analyst described Bitcoin (BTC-USD) as a “multinarrative asset” that responds to various macroeconomic forces simultaneously.

While concerns about interest rates staying “higher for longer” weigh on the crypto industry, Gazmararian pointed to a countervailing force: governments may “start working the money printers again” to fund defense spending during wartime, potentially driving liquidity that would support crypto and other risk assets.

Despite the recent gains, Gazmararian cautioned against reading too much into Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) short-term performance. The asset remains down 45% from its highs at the end of last year, and she emphasized that broader market cycles are driving Bitcoin’s trajectory more than any short-term reaction to geopolitical uncertainty.

Gazmararian characterized Bitcoin (BTC-USD) as a “programmed asset” that has followed predictable four-year cycles since its inception.

She highlighted that institutional adoption has been “absolutely incredible in the last two years,” contributing to shallower drawdowns compared to previous cycles as mainstream investors enter the market.

Looking ahead, the analyst suggested Bitcoin (BTC-USD) could drop to around the $50,000 mark later this year if drawdowns reach 60%, or potentially test the low $40,000s if historical cycles fully repeat.

While infrastructure continues to mature, Gazmararian concluded that Bitcoin (BTC-USD) remains a “cyclical nascent market” whose price movements continue to reflect historical patterns.

Bitcoin-backed ETFs: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), and (EZBC).
